section.heading: Pilot program for monitoring sexual offenders

subparagraph.heading: In general
subparagraph.chapeau: The Attorney General is authorized to award grants (referred to as “Jessica Lunsford and Sarah Lunde Grants”) to States, local governments, and Indian tribal governments to assist in—

clause.content: carrying out programs to outfit sex offenders with electronic monitoring units; and
the employment of law enforcement officials necessary to carry out such programs.

content.p: The Attorney General shall award grants under this section for a period not to exceed 3 years.

subparagraph.heading: Minimum standards
subparagraph.chapeau: The electronic monitoring units used in the pilot program shall at a minimum—

clause.content: provide a tracking device for each offender that contains a central processing unit with global positioning system; and
permit continuous monitoring of offenders 24 hours a day.

content.p: Each State, local government, or Indian tribal government desiring a grant under this section shall submit an application to the Attorney General at such time, in such manner, and accompanied by such information as the Attorney General may reasonably require.

subparagraph.heading: Contents
subparagraph.chapeau: Each application submitted pursuant to subparagraph (A) shall—

clause.content: describe the activities for which assistance under this section is sought; and
provide such additional assurances as the Attorney General determines to be essential to ensure compliance with the requirements of this section.

content.p: In making grants under this section, the Attorney General shall ensure that different approaches to monitoring are funded to allow an assessment of effectiveness.
There are authorized to be appropriated $5,000,000 for each of the fiscal years 2007 through 2009 to carry out this section.

paragraph.heading: Report
paragraph.chapeau: Not later than September 1, 2010, the Attorney General shall report to Congress—

subparagraph.content: assessing the effectiveness and value of this section;
comparing the cost effectiveness of the electronic monitoring to reduce sex offenses compared to other alternatives; and
making recommendations for continuing funding and the appropriate levels for such funding.

note.heading: Codification
note.p: Section was formerly classified to section 16981 of Title 42, The Public Health and Welfare, prior to editorial reclassification and renumbering as this section.

note.heading: Amendments
note.p: 2008—Subsec. (a)(1)(C). Pub. L. 110–400, § 4(a), amended subpar. (C) generally. Prior to amendment, subpar. (C) set minimum standards for electronic monitoring units used in the pilot program.

note.heading: Effective Date of 2008 Amendment
note.p: Pub. L. 110–400, § 4(b), Oct. 13, 2008, 122 Stat. 4228, provided that: “The amendment made by subsection (a) [amending this section] shall apply to grants provided on or after the date of the enactment of this Act [Oct. 13, 2008].”
